body: Dense pass complete. THREE findings, in order of what you asked about: (1) The live run (RUN_20260723_1811, SLS(92)) ran 112 min, both Phase 3B cycles ended STAND DOWN/OVERFIT (honest null, gates working correctly -- not a bug). Terminal-level log confirms a CLEAN shutdown at the end (Expert removed, MT4 stopped) -- not a crash. (2) You were right that there were real errors I missed: P2 regime-intelligence WR/IC values were corrupted (WR in the hundreds-of-millions-of-percent, IC with 70-300 digit magnitudes) -- root-caused as most likely a stale/mismatched saved-state file, fixed with sanitize-on-load guards (WARRANT#91, compiled 0 errors, not yet live-tested). (3) Bonus finding: the new watchdog script I wired in this session had ALREADY silently hung (Windows console QuickEdit Mode freeze) 1 minute after starting and never caught the terminal's death -- found and fixed with a kernel32 P/Invoke + stdin redirect, validated fresh-spawn but not against a real freeze event. The standing null-edge/double-WR ask needs real signal research, not a quick code fix -- flagged honestly rather than faked. Full detail in forge_ledger.json pass_73 and memory.
